_comment: Scheduling metadata for the CRUD scenarios in scenarios.json. Keeps service prerequisites and account quota kinds as DATA so a scheduler can serialize/parallelize lifecycles safely against core.budgets (vpc=5, private-dns=3, ...). 'quota_kinds' maps a lifecycle id -> the capped resource kinds it CREATES (and must reserve in core.budgets before running); a scheduler should serialize lifecycles that share a kind once the live usage + reserved slots reach the limit. 'prerequisites' lists the in-flow resource families each lifecycle stands up before its headline resource (informational: these are created inline by the lifecycle's own steps, not external preconditions). 'budget_paths' maps the create PATH that consumes a kind -> the kind, mirroring engine._budget_kind_for_path so the mapping has one source of truth.
notes: Quota kinds come from core.budgets.DEFAULT_LIMITS (vpc=5, private-dns=3). networking-dns-hosted-zone is currently disabled (needs a private-dns activate sub-flow) and so carries no quota row yet; when re-enabled it should declare private-dns. The four heavy lifecycles each stand up their own VPC inline, so under the 5-VPC cap a scheduler must serialize them (and the two light VPC lifecycles) rather than run them concurrently. The engine reserves a slot in core.budgets before each /v1/vpcs (and /v1/private-dns) create and releases it on teardown; a multi-process scheduler should first Budget.sync() each kind from a live list call, then gate concurrency on Budget.available(kind).
